key_risks: A reversal in AI or semiconductor leadership could sharply hurt the largest holdings.
Hot inflation data or hawkish FOMC communication could lift yields and compress high-growth equity multiples.
South Korea and Taiwan exposures carry currency, export-cycle, and regional geopolitical risks.
Regional banks could underperform if credit-quality or commercial real estate concerns resurface.

portfolio.rationale: Strongest AI-linked and semiconductor momentum in the universe, with large 7-day, 30-day, 6-month, and 1-year gains and a supportive risk-on tape.
Very strong recent and medium-term country momentum, with high sensitivity to the global semiconductor and export cycle.
Semiconductor supply-chain exposure with strong 30-day and 6-month performance, offering another route to AI hardware momentum.
Captures trend persistence across the strongest recent U.S. equity winners while diversifying beyond a single sector ETF.
Recent strong 7-day and 30-day performance, improving domestic growth signals, and potential benefit from a positively sloped yield curve.

portfolio_rationale: The allocation emphasizes the clearest one-month trend signals: semiconductors, AI hardware supply-chain countries, and momentum equities. Growth data remain resilient enough to support risk assets, while the expected Fed hold reduces near-term policy-shock risk.
rationale_summary: A concentrated risk-on portfolio is chosen to beat the S&P 500 over one month, led by semiconductor and related country momentum. A smaller regional-bank position adds exposure to domestic cyclical broadening outside mega-cap technology.
